approvedView.title: FAIR certification: Not certified
approvedView.certificationDescription: *Your repository is not certified yet. In the report below you can review the estimation to what extent your repository correspond to the FAIR principles.*

findable.title: Findable
findable.description: The first step in (re)using data is to find them. Metadata and data should be easy to find for both humans and computers. Machine-readable metadata are essential for automatic discovery of datasets and services, so this is an essential component of the FAIRification process.
findable.expandedDetail: Metadata and data should be easy to find for both humans and computers. Machine-readable metadata are essential for automatic discovery of datasets and services, so this is an essential component of the FAIRification process.

accessible.title: Accessible
accessible.description: Once the user finds the required data, she/he/they need to know how they can be accessed, possibly including authentication and authorisation.
accessible.expandedDetail: Once the user finds the required data, she/he/they need to know how they can be accessed, possibly including authentication and authorisation.

interoperable.title: Interoperable
interoperable.description: The data usually need to be integrated with other data. In addition, the data need to interoperate with applications or workflows for analysis, storage, and processing.
interoperable.expandedDetail: The data usually need to be integrated with other data. In addition, the data need to interoperate with applications or workflows for analysis, storage, and processing.

reusable.title: Reusable
reusable.description: The ultimate goal of FAIR is to optimise the reuse of data. To achieve this, metadata and data should be well-described so that they can be replicated and/or combined in different settings.
reusable.expandedDetail: The ultimate goal of FAIR is to optimise the reuse of data. To achieve this, metadata and data should be well-described so that they can be replicated and/or combined in different settings.

fitForFunders.title: Fit for funders
fitForFunders.description: The first step in (re)using data is to find them. Metadata and data should be easy to find for both humans and computers. Machine-readable metadata are essential for automatic discovery of datasets and services, so this is an essential component of the FAIRification process.
fitForFunders.expandedDetail: The first step in (re)using data is to find them. Metadata and data should be easy to find for both humans and computers. Machine-readable metadata are essential for automatic discovery of datasets and services, so this is an essential component of the FAIRification process.

fitForFuture.title: Fit for future
fitForFuture.description: The first step in (re)using data is to find them. Metadata and data should be easy to find for both humans and computers. Machine-readable metadata are essential for automatic discovery of datasets and services, so this is an essential component of the FAIRification process.

Machine-readable metadata are essential for automatic discovery of datasets and services, so this is an essential component of the FAIRification process.

operational.title: Operational / Environmental considerations
operational.description: We have run an automatic check of your repository. To get certification please answer following questions:
operational.questionRepositoryPolicy: Describe your repository’s data retention and access policy.
operational.questionEnvironmental: Describe any environmental or operational considerations relevant to your repository.
